Wrong reference: TICKS_PER_REV should be RobotConfig
Wrong reference: lastX should be lastSquare

bugs.fix: Changed to "Cross       : snap both to CLOSED"

bugs.error_message: Turret does full spin to limit immediately when tracking enabled
bugs.root_cause: Additive tracking update (current + tx * TICKS_PER_DEG) fires every loop while motor is still moving. Motor lag means tx stays large, target walks to MAX_TICKS/MIN_TICKS within a fraction of a second.
bugs.fix: Added TURRET_TRACKING_GAIN (0.15) to RobotConfig. Each loop now only applies 15% of the error as a P controller, preventing runaway target accumulation.

bugs.error_message: Turret angle display shows ~240 deg when physically at ~90 deg
bugs.root_cause: TICKS_PER_DEG used 5.2:1 gear ratio but motor was swapped to 13.7:1 (goBILDA 5203-2402-0004, 435 RPM). Factor of 13.7/5.2=2.63 explains 90*2.63=237~240 deg display.
bugs.fix: Updated TICKS_PER_DEG formula from 5.2 to 13.7 gearbox ratio. TURRET_MAX_TICKS still needs re-measuring on the robot.

bugs.error_message: Turret did near-full rotation during tracking, ignoring limits, nearly ripped cables
bugs.root_cause: Likely: setPower(0) on init disabled RUN_TO_POSITION PID; on re-enable motor ran open-loop briefly with large tx commanding full limit. Also TURRET_MAX_POWER=0.6 too high for unvalidated system.
bugs.fix: Keep power always on (PID always active). Added hard safety cutoff: if currentPosition exceeds TURRET_MAX_TICKS, halt tracking immediately. Reduced TURRET_MAX_POWER to 0.3 for testing.

bugs.error_message: Motor spinning at max power with tracking OFF when turret pushed manually
bugs.root_cause: Previous fix changed setPower(0) to setPower(TURRET_MAX_POWER) at init to keep PID active. This meant motor was always in RUN_TO_POSITION with full power, fighting any manual movement even when tracking was disabled.
bugs.fix: Reverted to setPower(0) at init. Motor only gets power when tracking is enabled. Toggle ON: snap target to current position then apply power. Toggle OFF / safety cutoff: cut power to 0 so turret moves freely.

bugs.error_message: Turret overshot limits and spun unsafely during testing; limit behavior was not reliably preventing outward drive
bugs.root_cause: Safety behavior depended mostly on clamped targets and did not combine directional blocking at the physical edge with a separate hard-limit cutoff margin. Test-mode speed selection was also not wired into turret control logic.
bugs.fix: Added layered safety: directional blocking at soft limits, hard-limit cutoff using TURRET_MAX_TICKS + TURRET_HARD_LIMIT_MARGIN_TICKS that cuts motor power, and explicit low-speed test mode power selection (TURRET_TEST_MODE/TURRET_TEST_POWER) in TestTurretController.

bugs.error_message: Inversion must always be on; turret still overshoots AprilTag and appears to run full speed instead of test speed
bugs.root_cause: Recent runtime inversion toggle introduced sign-path complexity, and turret target updates could still move too far per loop under large tx. Main TurretController also used TURRET_MAX_POWER directly, so test power settings did not affect that path.
bugs.fix: Removed inversion toggle and made tx inversion fixed in both controllers. Added per-loop target delta clamp via TURRET_MAX_STEP_TICKS to reduce overshoot. Updated TurretController to use configuredPower() so TURRET_TEST_MODE/TURRET_TEST_POWER apply there too. Corrected directional limit-block conditions for the fixed-inversion sign convention.

bugs.error_message: Need ultra-safe, very slow turret testing profile; speed is not priority
bugs.root_cause: Tracking updates and control arming were still too permissive for bring-up risk tolerance.
bugs.fix: Applied ultra-conservative constants (lower power, lower gain, larger deadband, 1 tick max step, 100 ms update period), required explicit tracking arm/disarm in TeleOp (Triangle), and defaulted turret power to 0 unless tracking is enabled.

bugs.error_message: Tracking does nothing until turret is manually pushed, then it runs away in pushed direction and seems to ignore AprilTag
bugs.root_cause: Tracking updates were based on current encoder position (current + delta), so manual movement could drag the command along and create unstable behavior. With conservative gain/step, integer truncation also produced zero-step commands near deadband.
bugs.fix: Changed tracking integration to update from target position (target + delta) instead of current position, and added a minimum signed 1-tick delta whenever tx is outside deadband.

bugs.error_message: Turret turns wrong way when tracking target
bugs.root_cause: tx was negated (-result.getTx()) but motor wiring/mounting means raw positive tx should drive positive ticks
bugs.fix: Removed negation: changed -result.getTx() to result.getTx() in update() and isAtLimit()

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: REVERSE should be FORWARD
Wrong reference: Telemetry should be TelemetryManager
Wrong reference: IDLE should be RAMPING

bugs.fix: Rewrote 3â†’4 lines (3 removed) | Also: double derivative = dtSecs > 0 ? (tx - lastTx) / d; + kD * derivative; | Also: integral     = 0;; lastTx       = 0; | Also: // Ziegler-Nichols PID (classic, halved for less a; tunedKp = 0.3  * Ku;

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Turret went past hard limit and refused to recover - motor stayed at 0
bugs.root_cause: Hard-limit block was placed at the bottom of update() after target-visibility checks. When past the hard limit the tag leaves the Limelight FOV, findTarget() returns null, and the motor is zeroed before the hard-limit recovery code is reached. Motor stays at 0 permanently until tracking is toggled.
bugs.fix: Moved hard-limit check to before any target/vision checks. When past hard limit, immediately apply -kF (too far right) or +kF (too far left) and return, forcing recovery toward center regardless of target visibility.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: TICKS_PER_DEG should be RobotConfig
Wrong reference: TICKS_PER_DEG should be RobotConfig
User requested removal of turret hard stop and soft-limit-only behavior
bugs.root_cause: Controller still had hard-limit recovery/cutoff logic and config margin, conflicting with desired soft-limit-only control strategy.
bugs.fix: Removed hard-limit branch/margin usage, deleted hard-cutoff handling in test OpMode, cleaned duplicate turret tick constants, and updated operator telemetry text to soft-limit wording.

bugs.error_message: Turret gets stuck at soft limit; motor hums with ~-0.7 power and does not move inward
bugs.root_cause: Software control path lacked an explicit breakaway floor for static friction and had no shared power-sign inversion setting, so commanded control direction could be opposite of physical direction at limits.
bugs.fix: Added TURRET_MIN_BREAKAWAY_POWER and TURRET_MOTOR_POWER_INVERTED config, mapped control power to motor power through the inversion setting in both controllers, and added limit/power diagnostics plus explicit idle zeroing in TestTurretController.

bugs.error_message: TeleOp reports 'soft limit' while moving away from limit; limit clipping semantics inconsistent with inversion
bugs.root_cause: Limit blocking and at-limit reporting used control-space sign checks, which can diverge from physical motor direction when inversion is active. Status method also inferred limit from tx/current instead of actual clip state.
bugs.fix: Moved limit clip checks to motor-space in both controllers and changed isAtLimit() to report only active outward clipping. Updated TeleOp wording to 'OUTWARD CLIP ACTIVE' to avoid lock semantics.

bugs.error_message: Turret direction still inverted in field testing (right positive, left negative expected)
bugs.root_cause: Configured TURRET_MOTOR_POWER_INVERTED did not match real motor/encoder sign convention.
bugs.fix: Set TURRET_MOTOR_POWER_INVERTED = true so runtime/test controllers map control direction to physical direction correctly.

bugs.error_message: Global motor inversion broke turret tracking direction; only limit side mapping needed inversion
bugs.root_cause: Direction inversion was applied to all control outputs, reversing normal tracking. Real issue was side association in limit clipping checks.
bugs.fix: Restored TURRET_MOTOR_POWER_INVERTED = false and flipped limit-side clip conditions in runtime/test controllers only.

bugs.error_message: Turret overshoots and becomes jumpy; breakaway floor applied even when not escaping a limit
bugs.root_cause: Minimum breakaway power clamp was applied to all non-zero commands, turning small PID corrections into large steps and destabilizing tracking near target.
bugs.fix: Changed breakaway logic to apply only when commanded direction is actively escaping from a reached limit; otherwise use raw PID+feedforward output.

bugs.error_message: Breakaway still activates immediately near limits instead of only when normal force fails to escape
bugs.root_cause: Breakaway condition was tied to escape direction at limit but did not verify failed progress over time.
bugs.fix: Added stall-gated escape logic with progress/time thresholds (TURRET_ESCAPE_PROGRESS_TICKS and TURRET_ESCAPE_STALL_MS); breakaway now applies only after insufficient escape progress.

bugs.error_message: Shooter pair does not spin up to velocity reliably; PID values appear wrong
bugs.root_cause: No in-repo automated process existed to estimate/update shooter velocity PIDF for the paired motors after hardware/config changes.
bugs.fix: Added TestShooterAutoTune OpMode that estimates feedforward per motor, sweeps P (and D when needed) for the pair, and outputs recommended shared PIDF values.

bugs.error_message: Turret stops dead when Limelight tracking is lost; should recenter to zero while tracking is enabled
bugs.root_cause: Loss-of-vision branches (`disconnected`, `invalid result`, `no target`) forced motor power to 0 and returned, so controller never generated a recenter command.
bugs.fix: Added recenter-on-loss mode that computes center error from encoder angle and runs through the same PID, soft-limit clipping, and escape/breakaway pipeline. Added explicit mode/state output reasons for telemetry.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling â€” exceptions would propagate uncaught

bugs.error_message: Failed to enable motor - you must set a position before switching to run to position mode
bugs.root_cause: Used RUN_TO_POSITION in shutdown recenter; mode switch from RUN_WITHOUT_ENCODER conflicts with SDK target-position requirement and is inconsistent with the rest of the controller which stays in RUN_WITHOUT_ENCODER and reads encoder only for feedback.
bugs.fix: Replaced with a manual P+KF loop in recenter() matching update()'s no-target branch; stays in RUN_WITHOUT_ENCODER, no setMode/setTargetPosition calls.

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: targetPreviouslyVisible should be recentering

bugs.fix: Rewrote 43â†’19 lines (11 removed) | Also: public final PathChain row2ToScore;; public final PathChain scoreToRow3;

Rewrote 18â†’26 lines (3 removed) | Also: /** Runs both motors together through the probe-po; private boolean identifyBothMotors() { | Also: * Only samples before 90% of vInf are used (residu; * samples below a 1 tps residual floor are dropped

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Shooter tuner: 'motors did not reach steady state' - ID failed with no visibility into which check tripped
bugs.root_cause: Three issues: (1) index-alignment bug - bottomTaus/topTaus lists drifted out of sync with bottomUVinf/topUVinf when tau fits failed on some probes, so the weighted-tau average paired wrong (u, vInf) with wrong tau. (2) ID_DURATION_MS=1800 and COOLDOWN_MS=1500 were too short for heavy 600g flywheels to settle. (3) Catch-all error message hid which specific check failed (vInf-below-threshold vs tau-fit-failed vs K<=0).
bugs.fix: Restructured identification around a ProbeResult struct keyed by probe index (fixes alignment bug). Extended ID_DURATION_MS to 2500 and COOLDOWN_MS to 2500 for heavy flywheels. Added 63.2%-crossing fallback (fitCrossingTau) when the log-linear fit has fewer than MIN_RISE_SAMPLES survivors. Added validateModel() to return a specific per-motor failure reason. On failure, now dumps per-probe vInf/tau/sample-count to telemetry plus diagnostic hints.
Changed to: if (!spike && elapsed > 100 && avgRpm > maxRpm)
Changed to "Drive from shoot pose to row 1"

Rewrote 7→5 lines (2 removed) | Also: /** In-place rotation at the shoot pose from goal-; public final PathChain rotateToIntakeHeading; | Also: /** Drive back to the shoot pose at constant intak; /** In-place rotation at the shoot pose from intak | Also: rotateToIntakeHeading = follower.pathBuilder(); .addPath(new BezierLine(p(41, 100), p(41, 100)))

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: returnToShoot should be retreatFromWall
AutoShortRange red-side autonomous mode existed but did not actually mirror path geometry from blue
bugs.root_cause: `AutoShortRange.Paths` constructor ignored alliance input and used hard-coded blue coordinates/headings; runOpMode also set a fixed blue start pose.
bugs.fix: Wired `isRed()` into `new Paths(follower, isRed())`, set follower start pose from `paths.startPose`, and refactored all stage path points/headings through mirror helpers `p(x,y)` and `h(degrees)`.

bugs.error_message: AutoShortRange staged paths drove only chassis; intake/flywheel/shoot actions were not attached
bugs.root_cause: `buildSequence()` added 12 drive-only steps and left mechanism flow in commented legacy block, so no spin-up or firing occurred during staged execution.
bugs.fix: Bound mechanism actions to staged steps: start flywheels+intake at stage 1, wait for spin-up, fire preload, fire again after stage 4 and stage 8 returns, and stop intake/flywheels after stage 12 park.

bugs.error_message: Shooting step did not explicitly enforce simultaneous flywheel + intake + gate at fire time
bugs.root_cause: `fireStep()` only opened the gate and assumed intake/flywheel were already running, which can break if mechanism state changes before a shot.
bugs.fix: Updated `fireStep()` onPreRun to call `startFlywheels()` and `startIntake()` before `gate.open()`, preserving wait-for-spinup step before first shot.

bugs.error_message: AutoShortRange timing did not match requested strategy (shoot 6s, pickup 2s)
bugs.root_cause: `FIRE_DURATION_MS` was 5000 and pickup stages had no minimum dwell-time enforcement.
bugs.fix: Set `FIRE_DURATION_MS` to 6000, kept `FLYWHEEL_SPINUP_MS` at 2000, added `PICKUP_DURATION_MS` = 2000, and applied `driveForAtLeast(...)` to pickup stages 7 and 11.

bugs.error_message: Intake engaged too late in AutoShortRange; should come on earlier
bugs.root_cause: Intake was first started in stage 1 pre-run, delaying collection readiness until that step began.
bugs.fix: Moved `startIntake()` to run immediately after `waitForStart()` and changed stage 1 pre-run to only `startFlywheels()`.

bugs.error_message: Red AutoShortRange moved toward goal at start; alliance mirror flipped both axes instead of a single axis
bugs.root_cause: Path mirroring used 180-degree field rotation (`x=144-x`, `y=144-y`) and heading `+180`, but this autonomous requires single-axis alliance reflection.
bugs.fix: Changed pose mirror to Y-only (`x` unchanged, `y=144-y`) and heading mirror to `360-degrees` for red side.

bugs.error_message: AutoShortRange briefly locked up at wall before retreating to shoot zone
bugs.root_cause: 2-second pickup dwell (`driveForAtLeast`) was applied to wall-hit steps (stages 7 and 11), forcing a timed pause before retreat path started.
bugs.fix: Moved `driveForAtLeast(..., PICKUP_DURATION_MS, ...)` from wall-hit stages to sweep stages (3, 6, 10), and made stages 7 and 11 normal drive steps for immediate retreat transition.

bugs.error_message: AutoShortRange did not guarantee turret recenter by autonomous end, causing poor teleop handoff
bugs.root_cause: Turret recenter only occurred on manual triangle press; there was no timed or end-of-auto automatic recenter path.
bugs.fix: Added timed recenter trigger at 30s-5s during sequence updates and a post-sequence recenter loop that runs until recentered or a hard deadline near autonomous end.

bugs.error_message: Fixed spinup wait could introduce avoidable autonomous dead time when flywheel reaches speed early
bugs.root_cause: `waitForFlywheelSpinup()` used only elapsed time (`2s`) rather than actual shooter velocity readiness.
bugs.fix: Changed spinup completion to velocity-based readiness check for both shooter motors with tolerance, plus a 2s timeout fallback for robustness.
